codeReview.summary: The change in lib/codegen.ml directly fixes the reported zero-value emission bug for generic nominal types, and the added regression test exercises a representative Result-return path that previously would have emitted invalid bare generic literals. I did not find any high-confidence actionable defects in the scoped implementation.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker discovered a still-open typechecker limitation while building the regression test: generic associated-function calls from outside impl scope lose concrete type arguments because concrete_type_for_name falls back to TStruct(name, []) / TEnum(name, []). This constraint materially shaped the final test design, but it is not reflected in shared library docs.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: lib/typecheck.ml:447-454 falls back to TStruct(name, [])/TEnum(name, []); the handoff's discoveredIssues section documents the same limitation with Box::new/Pair::new examples.

summary: Reviewed the handoff, transcript skeleton, skill guidance, shared-state docs, and commit 59c66d1. The feature appears correct and in-scope: generic zero values now preserve type arguments in codegen, and the added regression test covers a representative Result error-path scenario.
